# @zakkster/lite-hud

Single-canvas zero-GC perf overlay for browser applications.

## What it does

Renders a fixed-position canvas overlay with ring-buffer-backed tracks.
Any profiler pushes values via track.push(value); the HUD renders bars,
stats, and threshold coloring. stats.js replacement that allocates nothing
per frame.

## API

createHud(canvas?, options?) -- create HUD, auto-creates canvas if null.
  options: width, height, position, hotkey, zIndex, visible.

hud.track(name, options?) -- register a track, returns TrackHandle.
  options: label, unit, lo, hi, warnBelow, color, samples.

track.push(value) -- ZERO-GC HOT PATH. One typed-array write per call.
track.peek() -- last value.

hud.render() -- draw all tracks (cold path, call at 10-15Hz).
hud.resize() -- recompute canvas dims for current DPR (auto on DPR change).
hud.destroy() -- cleanup.
hud.visible -- get/set visibility.

## Sink pattern

Any profiler calls track.push(v) once per frame:
  fpsTrack.push(fps);
  frameTrack.push(dt);
  gcTrack.push(bytesPerCall);
  nodesTrack.push(activeNodes);

The HUD renders them all on one canvas with independent ring buffers.

## Threshold coloring

hi set + warnBelow false: green below hi, amber above (frame time, draws).
lo set + warnBelow true: green above lo, amber below (FPS, throughput).

## Zero dependencies

Single-file ESM. Canvas 2D only. No DOM manipulation on the hot path.
